subject: english
source_title: Third Term SS1 English Language Lesson Notes
learning_objectives: Identify the short and long front vowel sounds
Recognise spelling variants for each sound
Contrast minimal pairs containing the sounds

sections.heading: The Two Vowels
sections.content: The note treats /ɪ/ as a short vowel and /i:/ as a long vowel, with spelling variants drawn from many word examples.

sections.heading: Contrast Pairs
sections.content: Examples include hit/heat, hill/heel, sit/seat, sin/seen, dim/deem, will/wheel, list/least, dip/deep, lip/leap, fill/feel, and bitch/beach.
